items.title: New Caledonia election tests territory's future after unrest and deadlock

The possibility of independence from France remains the central political issue, with today’s vote taking place peacefully. The election comes two years after plans to expand voting rights to thousands of non-Indigenous residents triggered deadly unrest and forced the postponement of elections in 2024. The victory means nine of the tournament's ten African teams have advanced to the next round, joining South Africa, Morocco, Senegal, Ivory Coast, Cape Verde, Egypt, Algeria and Ghana. For some teams, including Congo, it marks the first time they have reached this stage of the competition.

items.title: Albania's "Flamingo revolution": Trump-linked luxury resort plan triggers mass protests
items.description: Protests in Albania entered their fifth week on Saturday, with demonstrations that began in May over a planned luxury resort project linked to the Trump family. Since then, the movement has expanded into a broader criticism of Prime Minister Edi Rama and his government, with protesters raising wider concerns about governance and political accountability. Vladimir Karaj, a journalist and editor at the Balkan Investigative Reporting Network, joins us from Tirana for further insights.

items.title: Armed conflict threatens Ethiopia’s ancient monasteries
items.description: Lake Tana in Ethiopia’s Amhara region was once a tourist hotspot, known for its picturesque shorelines and monasteries dating back to the 14th century. But since 2023, an ongoing war between the Fano militias and federal government forces has scared away the tourists. Cities are subject to shootings and grenade attacks, while rural areas face drone strikes and kidnappings for ransom. Now, the future of these ancient monasteries is uncertain. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

items.title: What France gets wrong about air conditioning
items.description: Is this the heatwave that finally changes French minds on air conditioning? Not the mind of environment minister Monique Barbut, for one, who said she was "horrified" by the idea of it becoming widespread. The rest of the world looked on perplexed as France suffered a brutal week, particularly for schools, hospitals and care homes. Long suspicious of it, French people are slowly adopting A/C, but it remains politically controversial and difficult to install in historic towns and cities.

items.title: Key poll in New Caledonia: First major vote on French Pacific islands since 2024 riots
items.description: Voting in the French overseas territory of New Caledonia closed on Sunday, with a turnout of 54.42 per cent, as citizens participated in the archipelago's first provincial elections since 2019. The results of the election, initially planned for 2024, will determine the balance of power in New Caledonia ahead of fresh negotiations with France on the territory's status, with independence remaining the defining political issue. Political scientist and post-colonial activist, Françoise Vergès, joins us for further insights.

items.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
items.description: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

items.title: Newborn baby rescued from rubble of Venezuela quake
items.description: Video shared on social media shows rescuers bringing the infant out to applause in the hardest-hit city of La Guaira, north of the capital Caracas. Leer más

items.title: Panic attacks and fractures
items.description: The BBC travelled to a Caracas hospital that is treating people from the worst affected areas.

items.title: Milei’s ‘Super RIGI’ investment bill approved by lower house
items.description: La Libertad Avanza’s beefed-up version of RIGI investment scheme offers improved tax breaks and benefits for investors splashing US$1-billion into hi-tech developing industries ; Bill now heads to Senate. Leer más

items.title: Defence seeks acquittal for former naval commander in ARA San Juan submarine trial
items.description: Prosecutors on Monday urged a five-year prison sentence for Claudio Villamide, commander of the submarine force at the time of the tragedy; Defence lawyers request acquittal, arguing there is no evidence linking his actions to the deaths of the crew Leer más

items.title: Key figure in South African police corruption scandal pleads guilty
items.description: Controversial businessman Vusimusi “Cat” Matlala admitted to fraud, corruption, and money laundering linked to a police service tender.

items.title: Iran attacks Bahrain and Kuwait, threatens to halt talks after fresh US strikes
items.description: Iran launched fresh drone and missile attacks on Bahrain and Kuwait on Sunday after new US airstrikes, while warning it would completely halt negotiations and close the Strait of Hormuz if Washington continues its attacks.

items.title: Germany considers 'opt-out' organ donor option
items.description: Germany is one of few European countries that still has an exclusively "opt-in" choice for organ donation — despite long waiting lists for organs. Now, parliament is debating a change.

items.title: Historic joy in Abidjan as Ivory Coast reach World Cup knockouts for first time
items.description: Ivory Coast reaches World Cup knockout stage for the first time after Nicolas Pépé’s brace beats Curaçao 2-0, sparking huge celebrations in Abidjan.

items.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy
items.description: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

items.title: Reports of Adorni's imminent departure from government ramp up
items.description: Rumours circulating that Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni will leave the government imminently as further lurid headlines emerge in the press. Leer más

items.title: New Caledonia heads to polls in vote delayed since 2024
items.description: Security has been high at polling stations as the South Pacific archipelago holds its first provincial elections since 2019. The vote in the French territory was originally set for 2024 but was delayed by violent unrest.

items.title: Venezuela races to find survivors
items.description: The search for survivors is entering a critical stage in Venezuela. NPR'S Eyder Peralta reports from La Guaira where rescue crews are still hoping to find people alive.

items.title: British Embassy scores with World Cup watch parties
items.description: UK Ambassador David Cairns hosts crowds for England-Ghana and Scotland-Brazil matches – just don’t mention the results… Leer más

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: United States hopes to get share of DR Congo copper production
items.description: The ore is used in many products including electronic devices like smartphones and computers, as well as electrical wiring, motors, and generators.

items.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
items.description: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

Germany, Denmark gripped by record temperatures as European heatwave moves east
items.description: Soaring temperatures put pressure on public infrastructure and dozens of deaths linked to heat

items.title: DR Congo files case against Rwanda at the International Court of Justice
items.description: Kinshasa, in a statement, accused Kigali of breaching international conventions, including those on genocide, racial discrimination, and torture.

items.title: Ukrainian strike ignites Russian oil refinery, as Putin acknowledges 'difficult period'
items.description: Debris from downed Ukrainian drones sparked a blaze at a major oil refinery in Russia on Sunday, according to local authorities. This comes as Russian President Vladimir Putin said that his country was “going through a difficult period”. In recent months, Ukraine has stepped up its long-range attacks on Russian military facilities, choking Moscow's fuel supplies and military deliveries.

items.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
items.description: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

items.title: Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence and Milei’s ethics
items.description: Milei has a militant and narrow world view that he’s tied to a vision that integrates AI and tech billionaires. Those same players have a global agenda that they will pursue, and Argentina will only be part of that plan for as long as it is useful for them. Leer más

items.title: NDLEA seizes N12.4bn cannabis shipment at Lagos port, arrests suspected traffickers
items.description: The NDLEA said the container departed Toronto, Canada, on 25 April and was tracked throughout its journey before arriving in Nigeria. The post NDLEA seizes N12.4bn cannabis shipment at Lagos port, arrests suspected traffickers appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Gamers gear up for GTA VI despite disappointment over digital-only edition
items.description: It’s only been on presale for a day and Grand Theft Auto VI is already predicted to be the highest-selling cultural product of all time. Fans were united in relief that the video game’s latest edition has seen a minimal price hike. But gamers aren’t happy that it doesn’t come with a disc.

Messi scores again as Argentina head into World Cup last 32 on a high
items.description: Skipper came off the bench to score his sixth goal of the tournament as Argentina warmed up for their World Cup knockout tie with Cape Verde by beating Jordan. Leer más

items.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban
items.description: Children under the age of 16 have been prevented from 10 key social media platforms in Australia since December 2025.

items.title: Trump warns Tehran as U.S. military strikes ‘multiple targets’ in Iran
items.description: U.S. President warns of a point where the U.S. may no longer be able to be reasonable “and will be forced to militarily complete the job."

items.title: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
items.description: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Canada reach last 16 with stoppage-time win over South Africa
items.description: Stephen Eustaquio struck deep into stoppage time to give Canada a dramatic 1-0 victory over South Africa on Sunday, sending the World Cup co-hosts into the last 16 for the first time in their history. Canada will face either the Netherlands or Morocco in Houston on July 4.

items.title: Turkey police detain dozens at LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul
items.description: Turkish police on Sunday detained at least 50 people, including a journalist, during a LGBTQ+ Pride event in Istanbul that went ahead despite a ban by local authorities. Homosexuality is not illegal in Turkey, but since 2015, the annual Pride march has been almost systematically banned and suppressed.

items.title: Messi rules the World Cup – but are Argentina too reliant on him?
items.description: Five goals in two games have confirmed the Argentine captain as the tournament's all-time leading scorer. But with Lautaro Martínez and Julián Álvarez yet to find the net, questions are growing about what happens if and when Messi's legs give out. Leer más

items.title: Court shutdown to end as Department of Justice resolves security dispute
items.description: Regional West Australian courts closed amid a stoush over security services are set to reopen about a month earlier than originally planned.

items.title: Japan fans flood Shibuya as World Cup draw with Sweden sets up Brazil clash
items.description: Japan reach World Cup last 32 after 1-1 draw with Sweden on 26 June, prompting celebrations across Tokyo. , sparking huge celebrations in Tokyo’s Shibuya. They now face Brazil in the knockout stage.
